summary: Japanese telecom giant NTT Inc. has drawn orders equivalent to more than €10.5 billion ($11.9 billion) for the euro and sterling portions of what could be the largest global bond sale by an Asian corporate this year.

summary: A closely-watched oil market indicator flipped into a bearish structure for the first time since February, as the US-Iran agreement to reopen the Strait of Hormuz boosts flows from the Middle East.

title: Amazon CEO Ramps Up Fast Commerce Push in Maiden India Trip
summary: Amazon.com Inc. is multiplying the Indian cities and towns covered by its Amazon Now quick deliveries, an aggressive move by Andy Jassy during his initial trip to India as chief executive to take on local leaders in a key growth market.

With renewables supplying a large share of generation and batteries being deployed at unprecedented scale, the country is offering an early look at how power systems operate when storage becomes a central part of the grid. Yet batteries’ success story is now ushering in new challenges, as growing competition erodes the arbitrage opportunities that drove the sector’s early growth. title: Europe Heat Wave Strains Power Supply, Sends Prices Soaring
summary: Europe’s electricity system is under mounting pressure from an intense heat wave, with extreme swings in power prices as soaring temperatures drive up demand and curb output.

title: WTI Falls Below $70 as More Ships Cross Hormuz After Peace Talks
summary: Oil fell as more tankers cross the Strait of Hormuz and signs of progress in US-Iran peace talks eased fears of an immediate supply crunch.

title: Zero Fed Rate Hikes Baked Into S&P Forecast, Says Wells Fargo's Cronk
summary: Darrell Cronk, Wells Fargo Wealth and Investment Management CIO, says zero interest rate hikes by the Federal Reserve are baked into their forecast that says the S&P 500 will reach 7,900 by the end of this year. He says markets are not in a selloff, he says this is a "rotation within equities." He speaks on "Bloomberg Surveillance." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Milei Shuns Wall Street to Raise Dollars at Rate Lower Than 7%
summary: Argentine Economy Minister Luis Caputo’s strategy of resisting calls to raise funds in international markets appears to have paid off.

title: Micron Earnings Take on New Gravity With Market on Edge Over AI
summary: Micron Technology Inc.’s earnings report on Wednesday afternoon is shaping up to be one of the most important in months as investors find themselves suddenly on edge over the sustainability of the AI rally.

title: Stocks Rise Before Micron’s Earnings as Oil Slides: Markets Wrap
summary: Stocks bounced after a rout driven by concerns over whether the unprecedented spending on artificial-intelligence will be justified, with traders gearing up for Micron Technology Inc.’s earnings report. Oil fell.

title: Musk’s SpaceX Adds Billions in Debt While Cutting Interest
summary: Elon Musk is no stranger to financial alchemy. His latest feat: adding billions of dollars of debt onto his cash-burning business empire while simultaneously cutting its annual interest burden.

title: Wendy’s Soars 42% Following Viral Reddit Post
summary: The meme-stock crowd is rallying behind another beaten up American icon — Wendy’s Co., the fast-food chain known for the Frosty, Dave’s Triple burger and the Biggie Deal.

title: Qualcomm to Buy Modular for $3.9 Billion to Help AI Push
summary: Qualcomm Inc. said it is acquiring Modular Inc., for about $3.9 billion in stock, adding artificial intelligence software capabilities that will aid its push into data centers.

title: Tech Stocks Lead Bounce After $1.3 Trillion Rout on Nasdaq 100
summary: US technology stocks rebounded, lifting key indexes, after the latest flareup of concerns about the scale of the artificial-intelligence-fueled rally wiped nearly $1.3 trillion from the market capitalization of Nasdaq 100 companies over the first two days of the week.

title: Trump Orders DOJ to Look Into Gasoline Prices
summary: US President Donald Trump ordered the Department of Justice to look into gasoline prices, saying in a Truth Social post, “Prices better start going down a lot faster than what I’m seeing!” Tyler Kendall reports on Bloomberg Television. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Hertz Slides After Profit Warning on Used Car ‘Softness’
summary: Hertz Global Holdings Inc. shares tumbled the most in two years after the car renter warned profit this quarter is trending toward the low end of its expectations and announced offerings of shares and notes.

title: Trump Says Justice Department Looking Into Gasoline Prices
summary: US President Donald Trump has ordered the Department of Justice to look into gasoline prices, which he says aren’t falling fast enough.

title: Gold Breaks Below $4,000 as Multi-Year Rally Grinds to a Halt
summary: Gold fell below $4,000 an ounce for the first time since November, as a resurgent dollar and the prospect of higher interest rates bring bullion’s three-year bull market to a halt.

title: Paramount’s $110 Billion Warner Bid on Track for EU Approval
summary: Paramount Skydance Corp.’s $110 billion takeover of Warner Bros. Discovery Inc. is on track for European Union approval, with the buyer prepared to allay concerns over film distribution flagged by the bloc’s competition chief Teresa Ribera.

title: JPMorgan Lifts S&P 500 Target to 7,800 as ‘Blue Sky’ Scenario Approaches
summary: JPMorgan Chase & Co. believes US stocks are approaching their “blue sky” scenario, highlighting stronger-than-expected earnings growth and a potential peace deal to end the Iran war as key drivers.

title: Bessent Says Iran Talks Feature Shift to Dollar Invoicing
summary: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent indicated that US talks with Iran include the longtime American adversary joining the dollar system and invoicing its oil sales in the greenback.

title: Hedge Funds Are Set to Win More Exemptions From EU ESG Rules
summary: Hedge funds are set to be excluded from a requirement that would force investment managers in Europe to categorize whether their products are ESG or not.

title: Bitcoin’s Retreating Retail Army Exposes Fresh Market Fragility
summary: Bitcoin spent years trying to shed its reputation as a playground for speculative retail traders. Its latest selloff is exposing the tradeoff: Wall Street brought scale and legitimacy, but the retail buyers who once helped absorb sharp declines have largely vanished.

title: Goldman Sachs Cuts Fourth-Quarter Crude Forecast to $80
summary: Daan Struyven, co-head of global commodities research at Goldman Sachs, explains the three reasons why oil prices are coming down so quickly following the US and Iran reaching an interim peace agreement and says analysts and investors “underappreciated the flexibility” in global oil markets during the conflict. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: The Future of Gene Editing with Jennifer Doudna
summary:
Emily Chang meets Nobel Prize-winning scientist and University of California, Berkeley, Professor Jennifer Doudna to discuss the origins of CRISPR, other breakthroughs in gene editing and what Silicon Valley gets wrong about the future of biology.

 (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Latin America’s Two Oil Giants Team Up in Search for Their Next Boost
summary: Petrobras and Pemex have agreed to work together to discover, produce and refine oil.

title: Rheinmetall Sinks as Germany Shelves Major Warship Contract
summary: Rheinmetall AG shares plunged Wednesday as Germany withdrew from a key warships contract, dealing a blow to investor confidence in the defense contractor following years of huge gains.

title: Canada’s AtkinsRealis Seeks US Approval for Nuclear Tech to Power AI Boom
summary: The surge in new data centers for artificial intelligence is translating into growth for the US electricity sector, and Canadian nuclear company AtkinsRealis Group Inc. wants in.

title: ‘Essex Boys’ Oil Traders Make Charity Offer to End UK Regulatory Probe
summary: A group of British commodity day traders have offered to donate £1 million ($1.3 million) to charity to close a UK competition probe that looked at whether the sharing of potentially sensitive information distorted markets.

title: Indonesia Plans to Boost Nickel Output, Fueling Price Drop
summary: Indonesia is poised to allow a significant rise in nickel-mine production later this year, providing relief for local smelters while weighing on global prices of the battery metal.

title: Dubai Holding Weighs Stake in Bain’s Data Center Group Hscale
summary: Dubai Holding is exploring an investment in private equity-backed data center builder Hscale, people familiar with the matter said, as it seeks to grow its European portfolio.

title: Stocks Rise Ahead of Micron Earnings; US, Iran Offer Conflicting Claims | Bloomberg Brief 6/24/2026
summary: US equity futures edge higher ahead of earnings results from Micron, after the Nasdaq 100 closed down by more than 3%. SK Hynix plans to raise $30 billion in a US listing to meet memory chip demand. Oil extends decline amid ongoing US-Iran talks. Max Kettner of HSBC sees an expiry date to US exceptionalism in the near term. (Source: Bloomberg)
